subject: science
title: science_11.json
description: Grade 4 Science Assessment - Day 11: Properties of Matter and Physical Changes

questions.q: What property of matter can be measured using a graduated cylinder filled with water?
options: Volume
Weight
Temperature
Magnetism
questions.hint: Think about measuring the amount of physical space an object occupies.
questions.explanation: Volume is the amount of three-dimensional space matter occupies, and it can be measured for liquids or irregular solids using water displacement in a graduated cylinder.

questions.q: Which of the following describes a purely physical change in matter?
options: Chopping a wooden log into smaller kindling pieces
Burning a piece of notebook paper in a campfire
An iron nail rusting after being left out in the rain
Baking a mixture of flour, sugar, and eggs into a cake
questions.answer: Chopping a wooden log into smaller kindling pieces
questions.hint: Look for the option where the appearance changes but no brand-new substance is formed.
questions.explanation: Physical changes alter the form or appearance of matter but do not transform the underlying substance into something chemically new.

questions.q: What scientific instrument or tool is used to measure the mass of an object?
questions.hint: It compares an unknown mass to a set of known standard weights, or uses two matching pans.
questions.explanation: A balance scale (or triple-beam balance) is used to accurately measure mass by balancing an object against standard metric units.

questions.q: If you place an unknown solid object into a tub of water and it sinks straight to the bottom, what does this tell you about the object's density?
options: The object is denser than the water.
The water is denser than the object.
The object has no mass or volume.
The object and water have identical densities.
questions.answer: The object is denser than the water.
questions.hint: Think about how heavy the molecules are packed together compared to the water around them.
questions.explanation: Objects sink in a fluid if their density (mass per unit volume) is greater than the density of the fluid.

questions.q: What state of matter has no fixed shape and no fixed volume, expanding completely to fill whatever container it is placed in?
options: Gas
Liquid
Solid
Ice
questions.hint: The air around you or the steam escaping a boiling kettle are examples.
questions.explanation: Gas particles have high kinetic energy and weak attractions, allowing them to move freely, break apart, and spread out to fill any volume.

questions.q: What is the term for the amount of matter packed inside a physical object?
questions.hint: Unlike weight, this measurement does not change even if you travel to the moon.
questions.explanation: Mass is a fundamental measurement of the total amount of matter or material contained within an object.

questions.q: What phase change occurs when a solid turns directly into a liquid due to an increase in thermal energy?
options: Melting
Freezing
Evaporation
Condensation
questions.hint: This is what happens to an ice cube left out on the kitchen counter.
questions.explanation: Melting is the physical process where adding heat increases particle movement, causing a solid to transition into a liquid.

questions.q: Why does a hot air balloon rise upward into the sky when the air inside the balloon is heated by a burner?
options: Heating the air makes it less dense than the cooler surrounding air.
The heat destroys the gravitational pull acting on the fabric balloon.
The fire creates extra mass that pushes down on the ground.
The air inside turns into a solid that bounces off atmospheric dust.
questions.answer: Heating the air makes it less dense than the cooler surrounding air.
questions.hint: Think about how thermal energy causes gas molecules to spread out, making them lighter per volume.
questions.explanation: As gas is heated, its molecules move faster and spread farther apart. This makes the warm air less dense than the cold air outside, causing the balloon to float upward.

questions.q: Which of the following is considered a mixture rather than a pure element or compound?
options: A bowl of salad containing lettuce, tomatoes, and croutons
A piece of solid gold jewelry
Pure distilled water inside a sterile vial
The carbon dioxide gas inside a sealed tank
questions.answer: A bowl of salad containing lettuce, tomatoes, and croutons
questions.hint: Look for something where you can still see and separate the individual, different pieces easily.
questions.explanation: A mixture is a material composed of two or more substances that are physically combined but not chemically bonded, meaning they retain their individual properties.

questions.q: What temperature on the Celsius scale is the boiling point of pure liquid water?
questions.hint: It is exactly one hundred units above the freezing point on the same scale.
questions.explanation: At sea level, pure liquid water boils and rapidly turns into steam at a standard temperature of 100 degrees Celsius.
